query: Someone hit me during an argument, but I had provoked them—what is the legal outcome?

phase8.query: Someone hit me during an argument, but I had provoked them—what is the legal outcome?

sub_queries.query: What is the legal definition of assault in the context of provocation?
What are the legal consequences of provoking someone to commit assault?

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text:
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise
than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be
punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may
extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand
rupees, or with both.
Explanation 1.—Grave and sudden provocation will not mitigate the
punishment for an offence under this section,—
(a) if the provocation is sought or voluntarily provoked by the
offender as an excuse for the offence; or
(b) if the provocation is given by anything done in obedience to the
law, or by a public servant, in the lawful exercise of the powers of such
public servant; or
(c) if the provocation is given by anything done in the lawful exercise
of the right of private defence.
Explanation 2.—Whether the provocation was grave and sudden
enough to mitigate the offence, is a question of fact.
Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of
his duty.

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending thereby to dishonour that person, otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Assault or criminal force in attempt to commit theft of property carried by a person.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 6: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
Section 133: Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour person, otherwise than on grave
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending thereby to dishonour that person, otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Assault or criminal force in attempt to commit theft of property carried by a person.

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, in attempting wrongfully to confine that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. Assault or criminal force on grave provocation.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 6: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
Section 135: Assault or criminal force in attempt to wrongfully confine a person.
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, in attempting wrongfully to confine that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. Assault or criminal force on grave provocation.

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text: Whether the provocation was grave and sudden enough to mitigate the offence, is a question of fact. Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of his duty.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 6: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
Section 131: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation.
Whether the provocation was grave and sudden enough to mitigate the offence, is a question of fact. Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of his duty.

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty as such public servant, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty as such public servant, or in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done by such person in the lawful discharge of his duty as such public servant, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour person, otherwise than on grave provocation.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 6: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
Section 132: Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of his duty.
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty as such public servant, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty as such public servant, or in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done by such person in the lawful discharge of his duty as such public servant, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour person, otherwise than on grave provocation.

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 6: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
Section 136: Assault or criminal force on grave provocation.
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, in attempting to commit theft on any property which that person is then wearing or carrying, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Assault or criminal force in attempt to wrongfully confine a person.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 6: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
Section 134: Assault or criminal force in attempt to commit theft of property carried by a person.
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, in attempting to commit theft on any property which that person is then wearing or carrying, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Assault or criminal force in attempt to wrongfully confine a person.

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 6: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
Section 131: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation.
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.

original.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
original.text:
Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or
knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any
person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or
preparation is about to use criminal force to that person, is said to commit
an assault.
Explanation.—Mere words do not amount to an assault. But the words
which a person uses may give to his gestures or preparation such a
meaning as may make those gestures or preparations amount to an
assault.
Illustrations.
(a) A shakes his fist at Z, intending or knowing it to be likely that he
may thereby cause Z to believe that A is about to strike Z. A has committed
an assault.
(b) A begins to unloose the muzzle of a ferocious dog, intending or
knowing it to be likely that he may thereby cause Z to believe that he is
about to cause the dog to attack Z. A has committed an assault upon Z.
(c) A takes up a stick, saying to Z, “I will give you a beating”. Here,
though the words used by A could in no case amount to an assault, and
though the mere gesture, unaccompanied by any other circumstances,
might not amount to an assault, the gesture explained by the words may
amount to an assault.
Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave
provocation.

Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation.

sub_query_2.chapter_title: OF CRIMINAL INTIMIDATION, INSULT, ANNOYANCE, DEFAMATION, ETC.
sub_query_2.text: Whoever intentionally insults in any manner, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Statements conducing to public mischief.
sub_query_2.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 19: OF CRIMINAL INTIMIDATION, INSULT, ANNOYANCE, DEFAMATION, ETC.
Section 352: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of peace.
Whoever intentionally insults in any manner, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Statements conducing to public mischief.

sub_query_2.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AGAINST THE PUBLIC TRANQUILLITY
sub_query_2.text: Whoever malignantly, or wantonly by doing anything which is illegal, gives provocation to any person intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause the offence of rioting to be committed, shall, if the offence of rioting be committed in consequence of such provocation, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both; and if the offence of rioting be not committed, with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both. Liability of owner, occupier, etc., of land on which an unlawful assembly or riot takes place.
sub_query_2.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 11: OF OFFENCES AGAINST THE PUBLIC TRANQUILLITY
Section 192: Wantonly giving provocation with intent to cause riot-if rioting be committed; if not
Whoever malignantly, or wantonly by doing anything which is illegal, gives provocation to any person intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause the offence of rioting to be committed, shall, if the offence of rioting be committed in consequence of such provocation, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both; and if the offence of rioting be not committed, with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both. Liability of owner, occupier, etc., of land on which an unlawful assembly or riot takes place.

results_without_global_rerank.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 6: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
Section 131: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation.
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.

results_without_global_rerank.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
results_without_global_rerank.text:
Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or
knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any
person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or
preparation is about to use criminal force to that person, is said to commit
an assault.
Explanation.—Mere words do not amount to an assault. But the words
which a person uses may give to his gestures or preparation such a
meaning as may make those gestures or preparations amount to an
assault.
Illustrations.
(a) A shakes his fist at Z, intending or knowing it to be likely that he
may thereby cause Z to believe that A is about to strike Z. A has committed
an assault.
(b) A begins to unloose the muzzle of a ferocious dog, intending or
knowing it to be likely that he may thereby cause Z to believe that he is
about to cause the dog to attack Z. A has committed an assault upon Z.
(c) A takes up a stick, saying to Z, “I will give you a beating”. Here,
though the words used by A could in no case amount to an assault, and
though the mere gesture, unaccompanied by any other circumstances,
might not amount to an assault, the gesture explained by the words may
amount to an assault.
Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave
provocation.

results_without_global_rerank.chapter_title: OF CRIMINAL INTIMIDATION, INSULT, ANNOYANCE, DEFAMATION, ETC.
results_without_global_rerank.text: Whoever intentionally insults in any manner, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Statements conducing to public mischief.
results_without_global_rerank.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 19: OF CRIMINAL INTIMIDATION, INSULT, ANNOYANCE, DEFAMATION, ETC.
Section 352: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of peace.
Whoever intentionally insults in any manner, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Statements conducing to public mischief.

results_with_global_rerank.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
results_with_global_rerank.text:
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise
than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be
punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may
extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand
rupees, or with both.
Explanation 1.—Grave and sudden provocation will not mitigate the
punishment for an offence under this section,—
(a) if the provocation is sought or voluntarily provoked by the
offender as an excuse for the offence; or
(b) if the provocation is given by anything done in obedience to the
law, or by a public servant, in the lawful exercise of the powers of such
public servant; or
(c) if the provocation is given by anything done in the lawful exercise
of the right of private defence.
Explanation 2.—Whether the provocation was grave and sudden
enough to mitigate the offence, is a question of fact.
Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of
his duty.

results_with_global_rerank.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY
results_with_global_rerank.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, in attempting wrongfully to confine that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. Assault or criminal force on grave provocation.

results_with_global_rerank.chapter_title: OF OFFENCES AFFECTING THE HUMAN BODY

phase11.final_answer: The retrieved evidence most strongly supports Section 131 (Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation.). Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine... Supporting detail: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend...
phase11.summary_answer: If you provoked the person who hit you, they may not be liable for assault under Section 131, as provocation can mitigate punishment.
phase11.detailed_answer: According to Section 131 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, a person who assaults another is punishable unless the assault was provoked by grave and sudden provocation from the victim. If you provoked the individual, this may be considered as not being 'grave and sudden provocation' that mitigates the offense. Therefore, the legal outcome may depend on whether the provocation was deemed to be sought or voluntarily provoked by you, which would not mitigate the punishment for the assailant. The punishment for assault otherwise than on grave provocation can be imprisonment for up to three months or a fine of up to one thousand rupees, or both.
citations: Section 131: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation.

reasoning_trace: Section 131 states that assault is punishable unless it is due to grave and sudden provocation.
Provocation sought or voluntarily provoked by the offender does not mitigate punishment.
The determination of whether provocation was grave and sudden is a question of fact.
caution_notes: Legal outcomes can vary based on specific circumstances and jurisdiction.
Consulting a legal professional for personalized advice is recommended.
